name: redesign-broken-intake-form
description: Patient intake page has audit gaps for a broken submit handler, missing form labels, and no inline validation. The UX proposal must address all three gap ids, produce a usable ASCII wireframe, and stick strictly to the project's existing library inventory (react + react-hook-form + tailwind — no new deps).

page.purpose: New patient signs up and submits their contact information.
page.userFunction: Fill out a contact form and submit to create a patient record.

gaps.description: Submit button is wired to an undefined handleSubmit — clicking does nothing and the patients insert never fires.
gaps.recommendation: Bind react-hook-form's handleSubmit(onSubmit) to the form element.

gaps.description: Inputs have visible <label> text but no `htmlFor`/`id` linking, so screen readers announce them as unlabeled.
gaps.recommendation: Add matching `htmlFor` and `id` attributes for every input.

gaps.description: No inline validation feedback — errors only surface as a generic toast after submit.
gaps.recommendation: Use react-hook-form's `formState.errors` to render per-field error text below each input.

expected.functionalSpec: Single-column intake form. Each field is a labeled input with htmlFor/id pairing for screen readers (closes g2). The form element binds `onSubmit={handleSubmit(onSubmit)}` from react-hook-form so the Submit button actually fires the patients insert mutation (closes g1). Inline error text renders directly under each input, driven by `formState.errors[<field>]`, in a smaller danger-tinted line so users see the problem without leaving the field (closes g3). Submit button is disabled while `formState.isSubmitting` is true. Empty/error/success states: empty inputs render as plain; invalid inputs render with a red border + error text; on success the form clears and a confirmation toast appears. Tailwind handles all styling — no new dependencies.
